React Beginner to Advanced Series — Part 5B
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Non-member ? click here to read.
In Part 5A, we learned that Props allow parent components to pass data to child components, making our UI reusable instead of hardcoded.
We created reusable components by passing simple values like strings and numbers.
But real-world React applications rarely pass just one string.
Imagine you’re building an e-commerce website.
A Product Card doesn’t need only a title.
It also needs:

• Product Image

• Price

• Category

• Rating

• Discount

• Stock Status

• Brand
Passing each value separately would quickly become difficult to manage.
Fortunately, React Props are powerful enough to handle complex data structures.
Let’s explore how professional React developers use Props every day.
Props Destructuring
